stories.headline: At least six killed as Russia launches overnight missile and drone attack on Ukraine
stories.summary: Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 drones at Ukraine overnight, a day after Kyiv sent drones across the border into Russia.

stories.headline: Russia pounds Kyiv for five hours in one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks
stories.summary: Attack on Ukraine’s capital and other cities leaves at least six people dead and dozens injuredRussia has carried out one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, launching a five-hour assault that left at least six people dead and dozens injured, and caused fires and damage across the capital.Ukrainian officials said the capital had been hit with about 40 Iskander-M and hypersonic Zircon missiles. stories.headline: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
stories.summary: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

stories.headline: At least 50 Malian soldiers killed in attack on military convoy
stories.summary: At least 50 Malian soldiers were killed after a joint Malian army and Russia's Africa Corps convoy was ambushed.

stories.headline: Why is China protesting about the nationalisation of British Steel?
stories.summary: The UK move to take over British Steel fuels a brewing dispute with China amid political leadership change in London.

stories.headline: US soldier killed and one injured after Iranian attack in Iraq
stories.summary: The US military reports another death a day after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan.

stories.headline: Why the World Cup half-time show may be a taste of things to come
stories.summary: The 2026 World Cup's spectacle is creating debate about the place of music in sport - and how it could infiltrate other tournaments.

stories.headline: Boeing says it will be ready to fund new plane programme by 2030
stories.summary: US group expects to have firepower to start work on 737 Max successor but warns that demand may be more of a constraint

stories.headline: Voters are struggling to keep up with Iran war’s twists and turns
stories.summary: Americans have grown frustrated with President Donald Trump over his handling of the war. Here’s what several said about how they’re processing the constant changes.

stories.headline: Iraqi-born singer Emmanuel Kelly among World Cup half-time show headliners
stories.summary: Abandoned as a baby and brought up by nuns in Baghdad, Kelly will be preforming alongside global megastars.

stories.headline: Iran attacks US Gulf allies, causing a fire at Kuwait power plant
stories.summary: Kuwait and Bahrain report several rounds of Iranian missile, drone attacks; sirens reportedly sound in Jordan.

stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has hinted he could reshuffle his government after Jens Spahn resigned as parliamentary leader, adding to pressure on the ruling conservatives ahead of key regional elections.

stories.headline: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
stories.summary: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

stories.headline: US day traders flock to ‘the most dangerous product in crypto’
stories.summary: Trump administration has opened American markets to highly leveraged perpetual futures

stories.headline: Burnham to maintain ban on North Sea exploration licences
stories.summary: Decision criticised by oil and gas industry as well as trade union

stories.headline: Aussie’s Slide Versus Kiwi to Slow on Rate Gap, Strategists Say
stories.summary: The Australian dollar’s steep selloff against its New Zealand counterpart is set to slow, with analysts saying Australia’s higher interest rates and a key technical support will cushion the currency.

stories.headline: Extreme Stock Swings Tempt Funds Into Reverse Dispersion Trade
stories.summary: Betting that individual stocks will be volatile while the S&P 500 stays relatively calm has been a popular and successful hedge fund strategy. But with the swings in share prices reaching extreme levels, the reverse trade is gaining traction with investors.

stories.headline: Prediction Markets Swell to 27% of Sports Bets During World Cup
stories.summary: Prediction-market trading has surged during a blowout World Cup, far outpacing the growth at traditional sportsbooks and driving home the competitive threat that companies like Kalshi now pose to the sports-gambling industry.

stories.headline: Safest Carry in Emerging Markets Is in Latin American Currencies
stories.summary: Emerging-market currency volatility has dropped to the lowest since the start of year and that’s re-energizing carry trades, particularly those involving Latin America that are seen to offer the juiciest returns.

stories.headline: Singapore Eyes Hedge Fund Tax Cuts to Boost Hub Role: FT
stories.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore has held talks with investment firms on reducing taxes for fund managers in a bid to maintain the island’s competitiveness and retain talent, the Financial Times reported.

stories.headline: South Korea Details Plan for Foreigners to Trade Won Easier
stories.summary: South Korea laid out a detailed plan to make the won freely tradable among foreigners, its boldest step yet to liberalize the forex market and bring the currency closer to full convertibility.

stories.headline: Korea’s AI-Heavy Market Now Sets the Tone for Global Stocks
stories.summary: For fund managers in London, New York and Tokyo, a new ritual has emerged before trading gets underway: checking South Korean stocks.

stories.headline: Federal Agents Were Told FBI Will No Longer Investigate ICE Confrontations
stories.summary: Senior officials deny such a change is in the works, despite guidance shared with agents around the country in recent days.

stories.headline: In Maine Senate Race, Jackson Sprints Ahead of Pack, as Bellows Drops Out
stories.summary: Troy Jackson, a progressive, solidified his edge over his rivals in an abbreviated nominating contest to replace Graham Platner. Shenna Bellows, another candidate, bowed out.

stories.headline: Trump Gutted Civil Rights Agency, Stifling U.S. Scrutiny of Workplace Bias
stories.summary: A little-known federal office spent decades investigating potential discrimination by government contractors — until it was stopped by the Trump administration.

stories.headline: Failed Immigration Cases Leave Chicago Prosecutor’s Office Reeling
stories.summary: Prosecutors have fled the U.S. attorney’s office in Chicago, angry about the cases they’ve been told to bring against protesters and immigrants.

stories.headline: In Maine, Troy Jackson Gains Momentum in Bid to Replace Platner
stories.summary: Troy Jackson, a progressive former State Senate president, showed signs of significant strength, as half the state’s counties picked delegates to a convention where they will choose Graham Platner’s replacement.

stories.headline: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
stories.summary: Iran has retaliated against US allies in the region, with Kuwait reporting that a power and water plant had been hit.

stories.headline: The classroom moment that bonds finalists De la Fuente & Scaloni forever
stories.summary: Spain manager Luis de la Fuente and Argentina boss Lionel Scaloni will go head-to-head in the World Cup final on Sunday - and the two men know each other well.

stories.headline: Cindy Burbank, Nebraska’s Democratic Senate Nominee, Files to Withdraw From Race
stories.summary: The move by Cindy Burbank could clear a path for Dan Osborn, an independent candidate backed by her party’s leadership. But Republicans have cried foul, and her effort could face obstacles.

stories.headline: Mike Lindell Not Registered to Vote in Minnesota, Where He is Running for Governor
stories.summary: President Trump endorsed Mike Lindell, a leader of the election denial movement, for Minnesota governor this week.

stories.headline: Second tanker in three months hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates
stories.summary: There have also been several unsuccessful attacks in a region where piracy appears to be making a comeback.

stories.headline: How these twins' rare growth disorder could provide the key to preventing cancer

stories.headline: Starmer pledges 'unwavering support' for Ukraine on final visit as PM
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister assures President Zelensky of the UK's continued support in its war with Russia.

stories.headline: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine

stories.headline: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'

stories.headline: Volunteer firefighter suspected of starting devastating France forest fire
stories.summary: President Macron said there would be no leniency for arsonists, as France - along with much of Europe - deals with a series of unusual heatwaves.

stories.summary: Chinese brands are meeting surging air-conditioner demand in Europe, a region with typically low ownership rates.

stories.headline: French MPs approve assisted dying law with strict rules after years of argument

stories.headline: Crackdown on 'objectionable' books in Indian-administered Kashmir sparks row
stories.summary: Authorities in Indian-administered Kashmir have asked educational institutions to review every book on their premises.
